html:
The Pretty Parasol is a Survival item. Like an Umbrella, it negates Sanity loss caused by the Rain when equipped. If playing as WX-78, the Pretty Parasol will prevent damage caused by Rain and prevent being struck by Lightning.

It requires 4 Twigs, 3 Cut Grass, and 6 Petals to craft and is available from the beginning of the game. It can also be used as a Fuel, providing the same burn time as a Log.

Like the Garland, the Pretty Parasol does not have durability but instead spoils over time.

Downloadable Content

In DLCs, the Pretty Parasol slows the rate at which the player gains Wetness by 50% and provides moderate protection from Overheating. It will no longer prevent WX-78 from being struck by Lightning.

In the Shipwrecked and Hamlet DLCs, only Wagstaff can construct the Pretty Parasol.

Usage

Tips

  • Due to its cheap cost, as a temporary measure, it can be an effective alternative to the Umbrella in Spring.

Trivia

  • The Pretty Parasol was first introduced in the Reign of Giants DLC and was also present in the Hamlet DLC. It was then added to the base game in the Hamlet Early Access beta branch on April 18, 2019.
  • The Pretty Parasol is unavailable for crafting in the Shipwrecked DLC unless playing as Wagstaff, which instead has the Tropical Parasol.

Gallery

  • Pretty Parasol Equipped.
  • A Pretty Parasol being used.
  • The "Add Fuel" option when placed over a fire.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

“This will keep my hair moderately dry, at least.”
–Wilson
“Pretty as can be!”
–Willow
“I do not like tiny umbrella.”
–Wolfgang
“The sun beats down.”

“MODERATE STRENGTH PROTECTIVE SCREEN”

“A dainty parasol that will provide moderate protection.”
–Wickerbottom
“That there could keep my beard moderately dry, eh?”
–Woodie
“It's more "pretty" than "dapper".”
–Maxwell
“Its translucency provides little protection.”
–Wagstaff
“I dislike flöwers, but I dislike wet armör möre.”
–Wigfrid
“It won't keep us from playing in puddles!”
–Webber
“This should keep me almost dry.”
–Walani
“A bit of shade is better than none.”
–Warly
“A lil water ne'er hurt nobody.”
–Woodlegs
“IT KEEP'TH WILBA DRY”
–Wilba
“Rain Taker made of friend hair”
–Wormwood
“It's pretty. Pretty useful.”
–Wheeler
“Better than nothing.”
–Winona
“A tool to keep the rain away.”
–Wortox
“Don't see what so pretty about it.”
–Wurt
“Flowers actually have lots of practical uses.”
–Walter
“Quite the afternoonified rain napper, isn't it?”
–Wanda

The '''Pretty Parasol''' is a [[Survival Tab|Survival]] item. Like an [[Umbrella]], it negates [[Sanity]] loss caused by the [[Rain]] when equipped. If playing as [[WX-78]], the Pretty Parasol will prevent damage caused by [[Rain]] and prevent being struck by [[Lightning]].

It requires 4 [[Twigs]], 3 [[Cut Grass]], and 6 [[Petals]] to craft and is available from the beginning of the game. It can also be used as a [[Fuel]], providing the same burn time as a [[Log]].

Like the [[Garland]], the Pretty Parasol does not have durability but instead spoils over time.
__NOTOC__
==[[File:Gift Icon.png|32px]] Downloadable Content==

In [[DLC]]s, the Pretty Parasol slows the rate at which the player gains [[Wetness]] by 50% and provides moderate protection from [[Overheating]]. It will no longer prevent [[WX-78]] from being struck by Lightning.

In the [[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|''Shipwrecked'']] and [[Don't Starve: Hamlet|''Hamlet'']] DLCs, only [[Wagstaff]] can construct the Pretty Parasol.

=={{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage==
{{Recipe
|item2 = Electrical Doodad
|count2 = 1
|item1 = Pretty Parasol
|count1 = 1
|result = Telebrella
|character = Wagstaff}}

=={{pic32|Prototype}} Tips==
*Due to its cheap cost, as a temporary measure, it can be an effective alternative to the Umbrella in [[Spring]].

=={{pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* The Pretty Parasol was first introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC and was also present in the ''Hamlet'' DLC. It was then added to the base game in the ''Hamlet'' Early Access beta branch on April 18, 2019.
* The Pretty Parasol is unavailable for crafting in the ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLC unless playing as Wagstaff, which instead has the [[Tropical Parasol]].
